tasks.task_description: Investigate migration logic trigger points in codebase
Examine database schema for has_migrated_to_kiro flag
Analyze backwards compatibility requirements for project-level configs
Check toolbox integration for kiro registration
Examine update process for app bundle name changes
Document findings and recommendations for each area

description: Investigate Amazon Q to Kiro migration requirements and implementation details
context: Found the main codebase structure. Key directories: crates/chat-cli (main CLI), crates/kiro-cli (new CLI), crates/fig_install (installation), crates/fig_settings (settings/database). Need to examine these for migration logic.

Examined database structure. The state table exists for storing key-value pairs, which can be used for the has_migrated_to_kiro flag. The kiro-cli main.rs already has migration compatibility handling with handle_migration_compatibility() function.

Reviewed installation script and fig_install crate. The installation script already uses BINARY_NAME="kiro-cli" and creates symlinks. The common.rs file has migration functions: replace_symlinks(), detect_dual_installation(), prompt_migration_choice(), backup_symlinks(), rollback_migration(), and cleanup_old_directories().

Found project-level config handling in context.rs - it looks for .amazonq/rules/**/*.md files. No .kiro directory handling found yet. Found toolbox references in several files including install_method.rs files.

Examined toolbox integration - it detects toolbox installation by checking if current_exe path contains .toolbox component. The update process in macos.rs supports changing app bundle names by checking same_bundle_name and using different installation paths.

Created comprehensive migration analysis document (MIGRATION_ANALYSIS.md) covering all 6 areas with current implementation status, recommendations, and next steps. The analysis shows most infrastructure is already implemented, with main gaps in project-level config fallback and toolbox integration.
